channel.title: Arti Metal – Electrolytic Copper Powder Knowledge Hub
channel.description: Technical insights, application guides, and industry knowledge on electrolytic copper powder from Arti Metal, Jaipur, India – manufacturer and exporter of high-purity dendritic copper powder.

item.title: Understanding Dendritic Morphology in Electrolytic Copper Powder
item.description: When you look at electrolytic copper powder under a microscope, you see a tree-like branching structure called dendritic morphology. This is the key to why electrolytic copper powder outperforms atomized powder in friction materials, powder metallurgy, diamond tools, and carbon brushes.
item.dc:creator: Arti Metal Technical Team

item.title: Complete Guide to Copper Powder in Friction Materials
item.description: Copper powder plays four critical roles in friction materials: thermal management (401 W/m·K conductivity), friction film formation (μ = 0.35–0.45), structural reinforcement, and noise dampening. Learn which grades work best for automotive, railway, and industrial brake applications.
item.dc:creator: Arti Metal Technical Team

item.title: Copper Powder in Carbon Brushes: Improving Conductivity and Service Life
item.description: Electrolytic copper powder (10–80% Cu) in carbon brush formulations dramatically reduces contact resistance, improves thermal management, and provides structural integrity. Dendritic particles interlock with graphite for superior bonding in motors, generators, and slip ring assemblies.
item.dc:creator: Arti Metal Technical Team

item.title: Choosing the Right Copper Powder Grade for Your Application
item.description: With six precision grades from A118 to A210, selecting the right electrolytic copper powder requires understanding apparent density, flowability, surface area, and application requirements. This guide walks through the decision criteria for each grade.
item.dc:creator: Arti Metal Technical Team

item.title: Quality Testing Standards for Metal Powders: IS vs ASTM Explained
item.description: IS 4848, IS 4840, IS 460, IS 440 and their ASTM equivalents B212, B213, B214, E53 – understanding what these standards mean, how tests are performed, and how to read a Certificate of Analysis for copper powder quality assurance.
item.dc:creator: Arti Metal Technical Team
